page: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-32GB is a virtual server instance built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, configured with 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. The CPU allocation is shared. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This server type does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate average multi-core performance but limited single-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 10%, while the multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores rank in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests show average read performance for both small and large blocks, though large block write bandwidth is weak, placing in the bottom 25%. Similarly, the PassMark memory score is weak. In application-specific tests, the instance achieves average results for Redis SET operations, software compilation, and text processing, but exhibits weak performance in static web serving throughput and medium-model LLM inference.

Given its shared CPU allocation and balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads that do not require dedicated CPU resources or sustained peak single-core performance. The hardware configuration and benchmark profile make it suitable for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and database operations that can tolerate shared CPU scheduling. It is less suited for high-throughput web serving, memory-write-intensive tasks, or real-time LLM inference.
description: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-32GB is a KVM-based virtual server utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with an AMD EPYC 7542 processor operating at 2.0 GHz, featuring 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores on a shared CPU allocation model. The instance includes 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core capabilities alongside weak single-core performance and memory write speeds. This server is best suited for general-purpose applications, multi-threaded processing, and database operations that align with a shared-resource model.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-32GB is a KVM virtual server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and no local storage, offering balanced multi-core performance for general-purpose workloads.

tagline: A balanced virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and 32 GB of RAM for general-purpose multi-threaded workloads.
